title: Native Pi TUI worker result-channel proof
goal: Prove, without replacing the current JSON-wrapper worker path, that a real interactive Pi TUI session can run in Herdr with a Khazad worker extension loaded per launch and submit authoritative worker output through a KD-owned artifact channel instead of terminal scraping.
acceptance: The existing daemon JSON-wrapper Pi worker path remains unchanged and remains the production/fallback path.

The Khazad worker extension exposes a terminating submit_worker_result tool that accepts the worker-result contract, validates basic required fields, writes an atomic JSON artifact at KHAZAD_WORKER_RESULT_PATH, and does not rely on terminal text, Herdr scrollback, or Herdr metadata.

submit_worker_result returns non-terminating validation failures for invalid worker output or slice-id mismatches so the Pi TUI worker can correct the tool call instead of silently producing bad evidence.
A bounded proof launcher can start a real Herdr-hosted Pi TUI with the worker extension loaded explicitly per launch and KD context passed through environment variables.

ask_operator remains available through daemon IPC when KD worker environment variables are present, and unavailable/timeout behavior remains a blocked-contract signal rather than invented operator intent.

Evidence documentation clearly separates what is proven by this slice from what remains unproven before any production replacement, especially daemon-owned timeout, cancellation, lifecycle integration, and packaging policy.
must_ask_if: The implementation would replace, default-enable, or remove the current JSON-wrapper worker path.
The implementation would treat terminal text, Herdr pane text, Herdr scrollback, Herdr agent state, or Pi TUI display state as authoritative worker result evidence.
The implementation would bypass daemon validation, scope checks, verification gates, repair budgets, cancellation, merge, or handoff state.
The implementation would make Herdr or a Pi UI extension required for headless/default worker execution.
The implementation would answer operator questions by typing into a worker pane instead of using KD daemon commands or ask_operator IPC.
